title: Manager, Student Talent Attraction and Development / West / 1 year contract
description:
Working hours: Mon to Thur (8.30am to 5.45pm) and Fri (8.30am to 5.15pm)

Salary: S$5,000 - 7,500

Location: West 

Duration: 1 year contract (extendable / convertible)

Student Talent Development Programmes:

  • Develop and implement operational strategies for the management of talent development programmes aimed at developing undergraduate student talents.

  • Work with colleges, schools, offices, and vendors to design and deliver training sessions and workshops, mentoring initiatives to enhance talent skills, and develop strategies to attract students into the programme.

  • Monitor the effectiveness of talent development programmes and make adjustments as needed.

  • Budgeting and expenditure tracking.

Talent Scholarship Application:

  • Collaborate with colleges, schools, and various offices to develop marketing and promotional strategies and materials.

  • Manage scholarship initiatives, including the application process, selection criteria, and identifying/inviting panellists of domain experts and interviews.

  • Establish ongoing relationships with domain experts/interview panellists and scholarship recipients, providing support during the application process and throughout the recipient’s educational journeys.

  • Maintain accurate records of scholarship recipients and programme impact.

Stakeholder Engagement:

  • Cultivate and maintain strong relationships with internal stakeholders, including senior management, colleges, schools, and department heads, to ensure alignment of initiatives with organizational goals.

  • Collaborate with external partners, such as educational institutions, industry experts, and sponsors, to leverage their expertise and support for initiatives.

Key Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a relevant field (e.g., Business Administration, Education, Event Management).

  • Minimum of 8 years of proven experience managing initiatives such as leadership programs, scholarships, or symposiums.

  • Possess strong project management skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks, meet deadlines, and manage multiple projects simultaneously.

  • Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, with the ability to interact with stakeholders at all levels.

  • Exceptional organizational and attention-to-detail abilities, ensuring high-quality execution of initiatives.

  • Proficiency in using project management tools and software.

  • Demonstrated ability to work independently, as well as in cross-functional teams.

  • A passion for education, student talent attraction and development, and fostering growth opportunities.

postedCompany.description:
PERSOL is Asia-Pacific’s leading Staffing and HR solutions partner, operating across 13 markets with deep local insight and regional scale. With more than 80 offices and decades of experience, we deliver integrated workforce solutions that are tailored, tech-enabled, and designed for the dynamic world of work.

We combine human expertise with smart technology to help organisations solve workforce challenges, unlock potential, and stay ahead of change. From recruitment and talent management to workforce strategy and advisory, our collaborative approach puts your goals at the centre.

In 2025, we came together under the PERSOL name — reflecting our bold vision for the future of work and our Group’s Vision: Work and Smile.

Whether you’re building teams, growing careers, or transforming how work gets done, we’re here.
